score_reasoning: 82/100. Full marks are withheld mainly for keyword coverage, not technical parsing risk: structure uses all-standard section headings ATS parsers reliably recognize (no penalty), formatting is clean and consistent with no bullet-glyph or table/column risk detectable in the text (no penalty). Deductions: -8 because 1 of 7 critical JD keywords (PM mentoring/management experience) has no evidence anywhere in the resume; -6 because a second critical keyword (SQL) is present only as a bare skill-list entry with no demonstrated bullet; -4 for two minor technical risks (em dash and en dash separators that some legacy ATS parsers mis-tokenize in header/date fields).

structure_assessment.notes: Name, location, email, phone, and LinkedIn present in a single pipe-separated header line — standard and reliably parsed.
Standard heading, but positioned last among content sections — see skills_placement_notes.
Standard heading, but content is a literal placeholder ('(none listed)') rather than being omitted.
Non-required but commonly recognized heading; fine to keep.

section_hierarchy_issues.issue: Skills section appears after Projects, near the very end of the document
section_hierarchy_issues.recommendation: Move Skills directly below Summary so JD-matching keywords appear earlier — both ATS ranking that weights earlier content and recruiters skimming top-down benefit.

parsing_compatibility_issues.issue: Em dash ('—') used to separate title, company, and location in work experience headers
parsing_compatibility_issues.location: All three work experience entry headers
parsing_compatibility_issues.recommendation: Replace with a comma or pipe ('|') — some legacy ATS field parsers mis-tokenize em dashes when splitting title/company/location.

parsing_compatibility_issues.issue: En dash ('–') used between start and end dates
parsing_compatibility_issues.location: All work experience and education date lines
parsing_compatibility_issues.recommendation: Replace with a plain hyphen ('-') for maximum compatibility with date-range parsers that look for a standard hyphen.

formatting_issues.issue: Certifications section contains literal placeholder text '(none listed)' instead of being omitted
formatting_issues.recommendation: Remove the section entirely — an empty/placeholder section adds no keyword value and reads as unfinished.

skills_placement_notes: Skills section is positioned near the end of the document (after Projects), meaning critical JD-matching keywords like SQL, RBI compliance basics, and Payments domain knowledge only appear after roughly 80% of the document.

Several JD-relevant terms are demonstrated in Work Experience bullets (e.g. RBI regulatory work, data-driven fraud model collaboration) but aren't mirrored as standalone Skills entries — duplicating them there would reinforce keyword matches without adding anything untrue.

keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: Work history spans May 2018–Present (Zomato APM through current Cashfree Senior PM role), ~8 years.
Cashfree Senior PM (June 2022–Present, ~4 years): 'roadmap of the risk and fraud detection product area', fraud scoring model bullet, RBI regulatory bullet.
keyword_coverage.recommendation: No action needed — exceeds the stated bar.

keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: 'Partnered with data science to introduce a fraud scoring model, cutting chargeback rate by 31%'

keyword_coverage.recommendation: Present but thin — add or edit a bullet showing SQL used for a specific decision (e.g. tying it to prioritization or fraud analysis already described elsewhere) rather than leaving it as a bare skill.

keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: 'Worked closely with compliance and legal teams on RBI regulatory requirements'; 'RBI compliance basics' in Skills.
keyword_coverage.recommendation: RBI coverage is present and the JD accepts 'RBI, PCI-DSS, or equivalent' — consider making the regulatory bullet more outcome-oriented (what changed as a result) rather than just descriptive.

keyword_coverage.keyword: Experience managing or mentoring other PMs
keyword_coverage.recommendation: No mentoring/management activity appears anywhere in the resume. If any exists (even informal), add a specific bullet. If genuinely absent, this cannot be manufactured by rewriting — flag as a real gap rather than optimizing around it.

keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: 'Responsible for writing PRDs and prioritizing the backlog for the partner tools squad' (Swiggy).
keyword_coverage.recommendation: Present but stated passively ('Responsible for') — rephrasing with an active verb would strengthen this without adding anything untrue.

keyword_coverage.keyword: Prior experience at a payments company, bank, or fintech
keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: Cashfree Payments is a fintech/payments company.
keyword_coverage.recommendation: No action needed — direct, strong match.

keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: 'Drove A/B experiments on the delivery partner app' (Swiggy); 'A/B testing' in Skills.

keyword_coverage.keyword: Exposure to Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar analytics tools
keyword_coverage.resume_evidence: 'Amplitude' explicitly listed in Skills — exact keyword match.

keyword_coverage.keyword: Technical background (CS degree or engineering experience)
keyword_coverage.recommendation: No action needed — exceeds the stated bar.
Genuinely absent and explicitly low-weight per the JD ('a bonus but not evaluated heavily') — not worth optimizing for.

keyword_coverage_summary: 10/12 JD keywords present, including 6 of 7 critical requirements. The one missing critical keyword (PM mentoring/management experience) is a real content gap, not something resume optimization can fabricate.
keyword_density_notes: 'RBI' appears twice and 'fraud' appears three times across the resume — reasonable, natural density for a fraud/risk-focused application, not stuffed.
'SQL' appears exactly once, only in the Skills list — thin for a JD that calls it a 'Must have' tied to a specific use case (self-service data pulls for prioritization).
No evidence of keyword stuffing — all keyword occurrences read naturally in context rather than as an inserted list.
readability_notes: Bullets are concise (mostly one line) with consistent tense per role (present tense for the current Cashfree role, past tense for prior roles) — good practice.
The Skills section as one dense comma-separated line is harder to visually scan than a categorized list, though this is a human-readability concern rather than an ATS parsing risk.
Domain terminology (KYC, chargeback, onboarding TAT) is used appropriately for a product-management audience without jargon overload.

prioritized_recommendations.issue: Critical JD keyword 'PM mentoring/management experience' has no evidence anywhere in the resume
prioritized_recommendations.recommendation: Add a specific bullet if any mentoring experience exists, even informal; if genuinely absent, treat as a real gap rather than something to paper over here.

prioritized_recommendations.issue: 'Must have' keyword SQL is present only as a bare skill, not demonstrated in any bullet
prioritized_recommendations.recommendation: Add or edit a bullet tying SQL to a specific decision already described elsewhere in the resume.

prioritized_recommendations.issue: Skills section buried at the end of the document, after Projects
Em dash and en dash characters used in headers and date ranges
prioritized_recommendations.location: All work experience entry headers and date lines
prioritized_recommendations.recommendation: Replace with a plain hyphen and pipe for maximum legacy-ATS-parser compatibility.

prioritized_recommendations.issue: Certifications section contains literal placeholder text '(none listed)'
prioritized_recommendations.recommendation: Remove the section entirely rather than including an empty placeholder.

prioritized_recommendations.issue: Skills listed as one dense comma-separated paragraph
prioritized_recommendations.recommendation: Group into categories (Product, Technical, Tools, Domain) for human scannability; ATS parsing risk itself is low.

human_readable_summary:
## ATS Optimization: Ananya Sharma vs. Senior PM — Risk & Fraud @ Razorpay

**Score: 82/100.** Technical ATS parsing risk is low — every section uses a standard, reliably-recognized heading, and formatting is clean and consistent. The score is held back almost entirely by keyword coverage against this specific JD, not by parsing/structure problems.

**Keyword coverage: 10/12, including 6 of 7 critical requirements.** The one missing critical keyword — PM mentoring/management experience — cannot be fixed by resume optimization; it's either true and needs a bullet, or it's a real gap for Gap Analysis to flag. SQL, a "Must have," is technically present but only as a bare skill-list entry with no demonstrating bullet — an easy, truthful strengthening opportunity.

**Quick wins that don't require adding anything untrue:** move Skills up near the top of the document, add a bullet demonstrating SQL in action, swap em/en dashes for plain hyphens and pipes, and drop the empty Certifications section.
limitations: This assessment is based on extracted resume text only — the source file's visual layout (columns, tables, text boxes, images, headers/footers) cannot be evaluated and could carry ATS parsing risk not visible here.
Exact page count/length cannot be determined from raw text; if the source document exceeds roughly one page for this seniority level, that could affect recruiter preferences not assessed here.

Regulatory-requirement coverage counts RBI experience only — PCI-DSS specifically is not mentioned anywhere in the resume, though the JD accepts 'RBI, PCI-DSS, or equivalent' as satisfying this requirement.
